methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt is a Seed-stage construction-technology company founded by Barrett Ames and Colin Devine, having raised $12.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector contains a sparse peer set; only two private companies (ICON and Apis Cor) operate in this space, with no public comparables available. The narrowness of the peer universe—particularly the absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum sample thresholds—constrains traditional multiples-based approaches.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($12.4M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $31M. This methodology reflects standard progression mechanics in early-stage venture investing, though its reliability depends on BotBuilt's execution and capital efficiency matching cohort norms.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $36M (high), with a median of $31M. However, this range rests on a single method, introducing meaningful uncertainty. Key variables affecting valuation include the pace and efficiency of capital deployment relative to milestones, competitive positioning within the fragmented construction-tech landscape, and the quality of early customer traction. A peer-median approach would require either narrower Housing & Construction comparables at equivalent stages or a broader sector sample; neither is currently available with sufficient rigor.
